briefs.title: Burnaby Council Votes on Rezoning, Housing, and Infrastructure Contracts — March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local business and development, including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage projects, a $4.2M water metering contract, and a modular housing pilot project that was sent back to staff for further review. Several rezoning bylaws were advanced to first and second reading, meaning they are still moving through the approval process and have not yet become law.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, monitor upcoming public hearings for your chance to comment.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Zoning Changes, Housing Plans, and New Bylaws at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and property owners, including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, new parking and fee bylaws now in effect, and a poverty reduction strategy. A modular housing pilot project was sent back to staff for further review, while two new zoning bylaw amendments advanced to early reading stages.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business is near 6535 Kingsway, 2757 Norland Ave, or 5058 Imperial St — rezoning is underway.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Parking Meter & EV Charging Meter Bylaw Amendment No. 1, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw Amendment No. 2, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw Amendment No. 2, 2026 — final adoption

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Solo Karaoke Liquor Licence, Parking Bylaw Changes & More
briefs.summary: At the March 10, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, several items were up for consideration, including a recommendation to support a liquor primary licence for Solo Karaoke at 6462 Kingsway, first readings of bylaws amending parking meter rules and city fees, and final adoption of updates to the Business Licence Bylaw. Council also referred a proposal for new off-street parking spaces near Pender Street back to staff for further review, with a report due within four months.
briefs.action_required: If you hold or are applying for a Burnaby business licence, check the Business Licence Bylaw amendments for changes that may affect you.

key_dates.label: Burnaby off-street parking Pender Street proposal — staff report back deadline

briefs.industries: Liquor, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll Confirmation
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Parcel Tax Roll Review Panel has voted to confirm and authenticate the 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll — a list of properties subject to a fixed sewer charge (a set fee applied to each parcel of land, regardless of property value). This decision is now set to go before full City Council for approval at the March 24, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property tax notice to see if a sewer parcel tax charge applies to your business property.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves water metering contract and advances several rezoning proposals
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several key items: awarding a $4.2 million contract for new water metering technology, advancing multiple rezoning applications to future readings, and referring a modular housing pilot project back to staff for further review. These decisions affect development activity, city infrastructure, and housing supply across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, watch for future public hearing notices and attend to have your say.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #24-23 Grand Promenade rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled
Burnaby REZ #25-09 6535 Kingsway rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled
Burnaby REZ #25-21 2757 Norland Avenue self-storage rezoning — First and Second Reading scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026–2030 Strategic Plan
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's (BHA) five-year Strategic Plan, which targets 1,000 new rental housing units started by 2030. Approving this plan is also a required step before the City can release $2,000,000 in Year 2 operating funding to the BHA.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 24 — this shapes rental housing supply in Burnaby through 2030.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BHA 2026–2030 Strategic Plan approval
BHA financial statements & 2026 budget expected on Council agenda
BHA first project milestone — minimum 50 unit starts deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving Burnaby's $36,250 UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on paying $36,250.57 in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — the organization that gives BC cities a shared voice when dealing with the provincial and federal governments. The money is already set aside in Burnaby's 2026 operating budget, so no new spending is being created.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $4.2M contract for Burnaby water metering technology
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $4.2 million contract (including taxes) to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water meters across the city. If approved, the first phase would install roughly 5,500 meters at meter-ready properties and 500 meters at homes with secondary suites — though residents would still be billed at flat annual rates for now, not by how much water they use.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 24, 2026 council decision — water meter installation may be coming to your property.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — water metering technology contract award

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.6M modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,626,050 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit rental home at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The City would own the building and rent out the units, with $350,000 in outside funding from FortisBC and the National Research Council reducing the City's share to about $2.28 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council meeting — this vote could signal more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award public meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on awarding 19 hired equipment contracts worth $29.8M
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services — things like dump trucks, excavators, tree chippers, and hydro-vac units — to support city crews with construction, maintenance, and emergency work. The proposed contracts would run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2029, with a total estimated value of $29,825,000 (about $9.9M per year), and could be extended for an additional two years.
briefs.action_required: If you run an equipment or contracting business, watch for future RFSOs — the next opportunity to bid may include a renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby hired equipment service contracts proposed start date
Burnaby hired equipment service contracts proposed end date
Burnaby Council vote on hired equipment contract awards

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewal with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving and concrete services for one more year, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2027. The proposed renewal is valued at an estimated $1,155,000 (including taxes), covering road repairs, storm sewer work, and watermain projects on an as-needed basis.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council decision if you work in paving or road construction in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby asphalt paving contract renewal period begins — if approved
Burnaby Council vote on Blackrete Paving contract renewal

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: New Mixed-Use and Self-Storage Rezoning Applications Advancing in Burnaby
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to move several rezoning applications forward — including a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway, a five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue, and a major amended mixed-use tower project near Grand Promenade. These are early steps in the approval process; each rezoning bylaw still needs further readings and final adoption before anything is approved.
briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming Council meetings for public hearing dates on these rezoning bylaws — your business or property may be affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Four High-Rise Towers with 1,574 Homes Up for Approval in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to allow staff to move forward with a rezoning application — a formal request to change the rules for what can be built on a property — for 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court in Burnaby. The proposal from developer Pinnacle International would replace a previously approved plan with four high-rise towers (up to 40 storeys) containing roughly 1,133 market strata units, 141 replacement rental units, 300 non-market affordable rental units, and ground-floor retail space.
briefs.action_required: Monitor future Council meetings for the full rezoning report and public hearing date.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Carrigan Court rezoning enquiry authorization

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on major mixed-use tower expansion at Lougheed
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application (REZ #24-23) that would allow developer Pinnacle International to expand its approved Lougheed-area project. The updated plan would add a 171-suite hotel, street-level restaurants, and 70 more residential units across two towers reaching 77 and 87 storeys — replacing a large portion of previously approved office space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this affects hotel, restaurant, and commercial space supply near Lougheed Town Centre.

key_dates.label: REZ #24-23 Burnaby rezoning bylaw — Final Adoption deadline

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway Up for Council Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to move forward with a rezoning application for 6535 Kingsway, where developer Intracorp wants to build a 12-storey building with 290 rental homes and ground-floor retail shops. If approved, the rezoning bylaw — the rule change that would allow this new building type on the site — would advance to its first formal readings at a future Council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — new retail and residential density on Kingsway could affect nearby businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-09 Kingsway rezoning — Council vote scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility at 2757 Norland Avenue Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application that would allow a five-storey self-storage facility to be built at 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the site would be rezoned from general industrial to a custom zoning category, making way for roughly 1,008 storage units and a ground-floor office — a resource that could support small businesses needing space for inventory or equipment.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote and rezoning bylaw readings — this could add local self-storage options for your business.

key_dates.label: REZ #25-21 Burnaby rezoning — final adoption deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Endorsing Burnaby's First Poverty Reduction Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing a new Poverty Reduction Strategy — a plan with 84 recommended actions across areas like housing, employment, health, and cost of living. If endorsed, staff would work with community partners to build an implementation plan, and the Mayor would write to the provincial government asking for ongoing funding to help cities carry out local poverty reduction work.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 24, 2026 — this could affect community funding and social service programs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy — Council endorsement vote

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Healthcare, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Consent Agenda: Building Permits, City Investments, and Burnaby 2050 Plan Updates
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council received several information-only reports as part of its Consent Agenda — covering building permit activity for January and February 2026, a year-end update on the city's 2025 investment program with a 2026 forecast, a status update on major civic construction projects, and an overview of how the city plans to put its Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (a long-term blueprint for how the city will grow and change) into action.
briefs.action_required: Review the Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan — it may shape future zoning and development rules affecting your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby building permit activity reports for January–February 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving two building permit activity reports covering January 1 to February 28, 2026. These reports summarize construction activity in Burnaby based on permits issued during that period and are being presented to Council for information only — no new rules or approvals are being decided.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby's 2025 Investment Results and 2026 Forecast
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on how Burnaby managed its $2.08 billion investment portfolio in 2025, earning $106.63 million at a 4.43% return. The report also forecasts a lower return of about $71 million (3.20%) for 2026, due to falling interest rates and a focus on keeping funds accessible for city projects.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — lower investment earnings may affect city budget priorities and local services.
briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Status Update on Major Burnaby Civic Construction Projects — March 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on seven major city-funded building projects across Burnaby, including a new recreation centre at Burnaby Lake, a new RCMP building, and several community centres. No new spending decisions are being made — this report is for information only, letting Council track progress, timelines, and budgets on projects already underway or in design.
briefs.action_required: Monitor project timelines if your business operates near construction sites on Joe Sakic Way, Sperling Ave, or Norland Ave.

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan Launch
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on the proposed launch of the Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan (IAP) — a roadmap that will spell out how the city's new long-term community plan gets put into action. The IAP would track progress, assign responsibilities across city departments, and set up a public online dashboard so residents and businesses can see how Burnaby is delivering on its planning goals.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final IAP release in early 2027 — it may affect zoning and development rules in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan — Council review expected

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Information Reports — March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a set of information reports at its March 24, 2026 meeting. No body text or specific report details were provided, so the exact topics under consideration are not yet known. Business owners should check the full agenda on the City of Burnaby's website for details that may affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby's website for the full agenda before the March 24, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council information reports review meeting date

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Advances Zoning Changes and Adopts Parking, Fees & Enforcement Bylaws
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council gave first and second reading — early approval steps — to three zoning amendment bylaws affecting properties on Lougheed Hwy, Springer Avenue, and Imperial Street. Council also gave final adoption to three bylaws updating parking meter rules, city fees and charges, and bylaw notice enforcement, meaning those changes are now moving toward taking effect.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business address falls near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, 2209–2225 Springer Ave, or 5058 Imperial St — rezoning could affect your lease or operations.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Three Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendments Up for First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council gave first and second reading — meaning early-stage approval, not final sign-off — to three proposed zoning bylaw amendments affecting properties at 6800 Lougheed Hwy, 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, and 5058 Imperial Street. These bylaws must still pass further readings and final adoption before they become law, so no changes are in effect yet.
briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming Council meetings for public hearings or final adoption votes on these three zoning bylaw amendments.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 20-storey mixed-use rental tower at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw (Bylaw No. 14802) that would allow a 20-storey mixed-use building at 5058 Imperial Street. The building would include market rental apartments and a double-height commercial space at street level, replacing a previously approved plan for a smaller six-storey rental building on the same site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and commercial leasing opportunities.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on rezoning and master plan update at 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the master plan for a site at 6800 Lougheed Hwy in Burnaby, known as BLAKE Village. The changes would revise the site layout, adjust building heights and massing, and expand open space — aligning the project with Burnaby's new long-term planning rules (OCP 2050). No new construction or land subdivision is proposed at this stage.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby Council meeting on 2026-03-24 to track whether this rezoning bylaw is approved.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 5, 2026 — Council vote scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: 49-Storey Tower with Retail at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a zoning bylaw amendment that would allow a 49-storey residential tower — including a 9-storey stepped base with street-level retail shops — to be built at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue in Burnaby. If approved, the rezoning (a change to the rules about what can be built on a piece of land) would follow a development plan prepared by FaulknerBrowns Architects.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby Council meeting agenda for the vote date if this development affects your business location.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Adopts Parking Meter, Fees, and Bylaw Enforcement Amendments
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Council meeting, three bylaws received final adoption — meaning they passed their last required vote and are now in force. These cover changes to parking meter and EV charging meter rules, updates to the City's consolidated fees and charges (what the City charges for various services and permits), and amendments to how bylaw notices are enforced. Business owners in Burnaby should check whether any updated fees or enforcement rules apply to their operations.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated fees and charges schedule to check if any new costs apply to your business permits or services.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update rules for public EV charging station fees and time limits
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would update how public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations are regulated in the city. The proposed changes would allow charging fees to be based on either time used or energy consumed (kilowatt-hours), update the technical definition of EV charging meters to include faster Level 3 chargers (up to 350 kW), and add a new rule that prohibits vehicles from staying in an EV charging space beyond a maximum time limit set by the City's Director of Engineering.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final adoption date — new EV charging fee structures and time limits could affect your business parking.

key_dates.label: Burnaby EV Charging Meter Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 — final adoption vote

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging fees at public charging stations
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would update the fees charged at Burnaby's public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The proposed bylaw would set new rates based on either energy used (per kWh) or time spent charging — for example, $0.2865/kWh at Level 2 stations and $0.3479/kWh at Level 3 stations, plus idle fees if a vehicle stays plugged in after charging is done.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business uses or offers public EV charging — these new rates may affect your costs or customer experience.

key_dates.label: Burnaby EV charging fee bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to add fine for overstaying time limit at public EV charging spots
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would add a new fine for drivers who exceed the maximum time limit at a public electric vehicle (EV) charging meter space. Under the proposal, the penalty would be $75, reduced to $60 if paid early — the same structure used for other parking violations in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: If your business has or uses public EV charging spaces, watch for the council vote and note the new fine amounts.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby correspondence items — March 24, 2026 meeting
briefs.summary: Council is set to review a correspondence item submitted for their decision at the March 24, 2026 meeting. Additional correspondence has been published in a separate public package for that same date. No specific subject matter is detailed in this agenda entry.
briefs.action_required: Check the full March 24, 2026 Council Correspondence package for details that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council correspondence decision — meeting date

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing 20-Storey Development at 5058 Imperial Street, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a petition signed by 175 residents opposing a proposed 20-storey building at 5058 Imperial Street in Burnaby (rezoning application REZ #25-13). The petition — which has not yet been voted on — asks Council to pause the rezoning process, citing concerns about blocked views, neighbourhood safety, and impacts on nearby residents' retirement security.
briefs.action_required: Attend the March 24, 2026 Council meeting to follow the discussion on this rezoning proposal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — REZ #25-13 petition considered

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Councillor Alison Gu announces she will not seek re-election in October 2026
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, Councillor Alison Gu announced that she will not be running for re-election when her current term ends in October 2026. She reflected on her work over the past four years, including affordable housing, climate action, tenant protections, and cycling infrastructure. Her Council colleagues acknowledged her contributions and said her absence will be felt.
briefs.action_required: If Councillor Gu's policy priorities affect your business, engage with Council now while she remains in office.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Recognize Joanne Morgan as Burnaby's 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to approve Joanne Morgan as the 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year and present her with the Kushiro Cup at the City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026. This is a civic honours decision with no direct regulatory impact on local businesses.

key_dates.label: Kushiro Cup presentation at Burnaby City Appreciation Dinner

briefs.industries: Professional Services
